description:
Transform the raw linear FITS file at ~/AstroImages/raw/uit_galaxy_sample.fits into an annotated, false-color PNG presentation image. 

Expected steps:
1. Open the FITS file in AstroImageJ.
2. Apply a contrast stretch (e.g., using Log or Brightness/Contrast) so the galaxy's extended features are clearly visible.
3. Apply the 'Fire' Lookup Table (Image > Lookup Tables > Fire) to give it a warm thermal color scale.
4. Use the Text tool to add the label 'UIT Galaxy Target' to the image.
5. Use the Scale Bar tool (Analyze > Tools > Scale Bar) to add a 50 pixel scale bar, labeled '50 px'.
6. Flatten the image (Image > Overlay > Flatten) to bake the annotations into the pixels.
7. Save the final processed image as a PNG to ~/AstroImages/processed/uit_presentation.png.
8. Keep the AstroImageJ window open so the workflow can be verified.
